teams.name: Boston Fleet
teams.firstGameNote: Hosted the league's third-ever game on January 3, 2024 and lost 3–2 to Minnesota — then lost the first Walter Cup Final to them in five that spring.

teams.name: Minnesota Frost
teams.lineage: PWHL Minnesota (2024) → Minnesota Frost (2024– ); the inaugural 2024 Walter Cup was won as PWHL Minnesota

teams.name: New York Sirens
teams.firstGameNote: Won the first game in PWHL history 4–0 at Toronto on New Year's Day 2024 — Ella Shelton's league-first goal remains the franchise's most famous moment.
teams.lineage: PWHL New York (2024) → New York Sirens (2024– )

teams.name: Ottawa Charge
teams.firstGameNote: Lost their January 2, 2024 opener 3–2 in overtime before a record 8,318 fans, then dropped back-to-back Walter Cup Finals in 2025 and 2026 — the second one ending on home ice.

teams.name: Seattle Torrent
teams.firstGameNote: Lost their November 21, 2025 debut 4–3 in overtime to Vancouver — Julia Gosling scored the first goal in franchise history, and the rivals up north still spoiled the night.

teams.name: Toronto Sceptres
teams.firstGameNote: Hosted the league's very first game on January 1, 2024 and lost 4–0 — a sold-out Mattamy crowd witnessed history, just none of it scored by the home team.

teams.name: Vancouver Goldeneyes
teams.firstGameNote: Beat Seattle 4–3 in overtime before a sold-out Pacific Coliseum on November 21, 2025 — the first PWHL team to win its debut on home ice; the Cup, however, went to Montréal.
